items.NAME: 🏓 Pong
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The classic that started it all — reimagined as a programming challenge. Pong strips game design down to its purest form: a ball, two paddles, and the code you write to control them. Players write real code to drive their paddle's AI, learning fundamentals of game loops, input handling, and physics prediction along the way.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Pong runs as a WebGL build served directly from the site. The interpreter evaluates player-written code each frame to control paddle movement, enabling experimentation with prediction algorithms, reaction timing, and strategic positioning — all through actual programming.

items.NAME: 🎹 Midi
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Music meets programming. Midi turns musical composition into a coding challenge — players write code to generate, sequence, and manipulate MIDI events in real time. Learn about audio programming, event-driven systems, and procedural generation while making actual music.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, the Midi game exposes a programming interface for MIDI I/O, note sequencing, and haptic feedback. Players write programs that compose melodies, build chord progressions, and react to musical input — bridging the gap between creative expression and computational thinking.

items.NAME: 🪖 Tanks
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Program your tank, outsmart the enemy. Tanks is a tactical AI programming game where players write code to control their tank's movement, aiming, and firing systems. Victory goes to the best algorithm, not the fastest reflexes.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Tanks pits player-coded AIs against each other on a battlefield. Players program targeting algorithms, pathfinding routines, and evasion strategies. The game teaches real-world concepts like vector math, state machines, and autonomous decision-making — all through the lens of tank combat.

items.NAME: 🐍 Snake
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The longer you survive, the harder it gets — just like real engineering. Snake turns the iconic arcade loop into a programming playground where players write code to navigate an ever-growing tail through tightening spaces. One wrong move and you're debugging a collision.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Snake lets players program their own pathfinding and decision-making algorithms. Each frame, your code decides the next direction — balancing food collection against self-avoidance. It's a crash course in state management, greedy algorithms, and thinking ahead.

items.NAME: 🐦 Bird
items.TEXT:
The Idea

One tap keeps you airborne — but code keeps you alive. Bird distills the infinite runner into a pure timing challenge where players program their own flap controller. Gravity is constant, pipes are relentless, and your algorithm is the only thing between flight and freefall.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Bird exposes pipe positions and bird velocity to player-written code. Players program when to flap using prediction, threshold logic, or even neural-net-style weightings — learning physics simulation, event timing, and reactive control systems one gap at a time.

items.NAME: ☄️ Asteroids
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Space doesn't care about your feelings — only your vectors. Asteroids throws players into a wraparound arena of drifting debris where survival depends on the thrust, rotation, and firing code you write. No friction, no mercy, no hand-holding.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Asteroids gives players programmatic control over ship thrust, rotation, and projectile firing. Write code that calculates intercept angles, manages momentum in frictionless space, and prioritizes threats — a masterclass in trigonometry, velocity math, and real-time decision-making.

items.NAME: 🧱 Tetris
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Every piece is a decision. Every gap is a bug. Tetris transforms the world's most-played puzzle into a programming arena where players write code to place, rotate, and drop tetrominoes. Clear lines with algorithms, not reflexes.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Tetris exposes the board state, current piece, and next queue to player-written code. Players program placement strategies — from simple column-height heuristics to full lookahead search — learning optimization, heuristic design, and the art of making fast, good-enough decisions under pressure.

items.NAME: ⛃ Checkers
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Simple rules, deep strategy — and your code makes every move. Checkers distills adversarial AI into its purest form: capture, king, and conquer the board with the algorithm you write. No hidden information, no luck — just your logic versus theirs.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Checkers exposes the full board state to player-written code each turn. Players program move evaluation, multi-jump chains, and king promotion strategies — learning minimax search, heuristic scoring, and the foundations of game-tree AI one hop at a time.

items.NAME: ♟️ Chess
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The ultimate test of computational thinking — now yours to program. Chess puts the full 64-square battlefield under your code's control. Write the engine that sees three moves ahead, or thirty. Every grandmaster strategy is just an algorithm waiting to be written.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Chess provides the complete board state, legal move generation, and turn control to player-written code. Players implement everything from simple piece-value heuristics to alpha-beta pruning and positional evaluation — a deep dive into search algorithms, optimization, and strategic abstraction.

items.NAME: 💥 Breakout
items.TEXT:
The Idea

One paddle. One ball. A wall of bricks. Your code decides what survives. Breakout turns brick-busting into a precision programming challenge where players write the paddle AI that tracks, predicts, and clears every last block.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Breakout exposes ball position, velocity, and brick layout to player-written code. Players program paddle movement using trajectory prediction, bounce angle math, and target prioritization — learning physics simulation, interpolation, and real-time control one brick at a time.

items.NAME: 🔴 Connect 4
items.MOTTO: Four in a Row of Code
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Drop a disc, build a strategy. Connect 4 turns the classic vertical grid into a battleground for adversarial AI — players write code that picks the winning column before the opponent does. Simple to learn, deceptively hard to master.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Connect 4 gives player-written code the full board state and asks for one thing: which column to drop in. Players program threat detection, fork setups, and lookahead search — learning game-tree evaluation, pattern recognition, and the art of blocking while attacking.

items.NAME: 🏴‍☠️ SeaRäuber
items.TEXT:
Logic Meets Fate

In SeaRäuber, logical mastery is only the first chapter of the story. While your code builds ships, directs fleets, and governs an empire, fate's hand — revealed through tarot — challenges your carefully laid plans. Will prophecy bring prosperity or disaster? Each card draw brings new challenges, forcing you to adapt, overcome, or embrace the chaos. In this world, strategy bends to fate, and only those who balance logic and fortune will leave a lasting legacy.

items.NAME: ☄ BitNaughts
items.TEXT:
The Idea

BitNaughts is a free-to-play educational programming video game: code gamified. Construct your object-oriented spaceship, learn programming to code your systems, and save the Earth from Martian invasion. Born from Iterate's interpreter and refined across four consecutive Microsoft Hackathons, BitNaughts turns real coding into real gameplay.

How It Works

Built in Unity 6.0 with C#, the project is organized as a set of Git submodules: a Unity project (bitnaughts.unity), a custom C# interpreter (bitnaughts.interpreter) that steps through each OP Code of abstracted languages, a WebGL front-end (bitnaughts.github.io) for browser-based play, a serverless Azure back-end (bitnaughts.mainframe) for persistence, and separate repos for assets, UI/UX, and components. Players write actual code to control their ship's systems (navigation, weapons, shields) with the interpreter evaluating their programs in real time against the game's physics and combat engine.

The Grind

Four Microsoft Hackathon pitches ('20, '21, '22, '23) shaped the vision through iterative live-demo feedback, each year pushing the architecture further: from a monolithic prototype to a fully modular, open-source game engine. The submodule architecture means contributors can clone, modify, and submit changes to any layer independently. The core challenge remains the same one Iterate cracked: making programming genuinely fun, not just gamified busywork.

items.NAME: 🔄 Iterate
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Iterate is a mobile code editor that uses Java/Arduino syntax but lets you build programs through guided tap-based selections instead of raw typing. It sits between block-based tools like Scratch and writing real code. Built for UC Merced's CITRIS Mobile App Challenge.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, the app runs on a 1,300+ line CodeManager that acts as both editor and interpreter. Users tap a line to enter edit mode, then construct code through cascading option menus: Create Variable → Integer/Double/Boolean/String → name it → assign a value. Flow control (if/else, while, for loops), Arduino hardware calls (pinMode, digitalWrite, analogRead, analogWrite, Serial.begin/print/println, servo.attach/write, delay, map), and variable scoping are all handled through the same tap-driven UI. A ColorCoder class provides real-time syntax highlighting (keywords in blue, objects in teal, strings in amber, comments in green) by injecting Unity rich text color tags on every frame. Pre-built lesson templates (Windmill, Servos, Stopwatch) load from Resources as starting scaffolds.

The Grind

The editor tracks variable scope by scanning every line above the cursor for declarations, dynamically populating only valid options at each step; you cannot write a syntax error. The whole thing compiles to WebGL for in-browser play on any device. $5,000 Grand Prize at the 2017 CITRIS Mobile App Challenge.
